summary.snippet: "It’s a pleasant experience to see the Doctor and staff at the desk"
"Bystander people watched my son crying and touching his stomach."
"I’m talking about salmon, Mac & cheese, broccoli, beef ribs and more."

most_relevant.description:
3 years ago I had a mitral valve replacement done at this hospital.  The care I received was beyond excellent. The nursing staff was kind and caring. The room I had was like in a 5 star hotel.
My doctors were superb and my recovery and time there was a pleasant experience all around. The first time in my life I felt so comfortable in a hospital.
I think that says it all and that was heart surgery done minimally invasive Robotic assisted.  For that reason and not having to be cut wide open was why I chose NY Langone.  The Tisch Hospital staff and I recommend it if you need a serious surgery for sure. Thank you all for such good care.

I’ve been a patient at Langone for several months now for my chronic pain condition. Dr Chui and Dr FayeMarie and the entire team have been superb. Great bedside manner and availability. Everyone is kind and knowledgeable and these surgeries have helped me so much.

Best hospital in nyc I’ve had 4 of my children here labor and delivery and postpartum are amazing the whole entire hospital is amazing great staff and great people throughout. Me and my family’s care is through nyu facilities and we wouldn’t change it for the world best doctors and everything more!!! Thank you for all your care and support nyu!!!

At times, plans appeared to move forward without clear confirmation that a safe and appropriate discharge option was in place, and explanations for placement decisions were not consistently transparent. For patients with complex medical needs and housing instability, discharge planning should prioritize safety, continuity of care, and clear communication. I hope the hospital reviews how these situations are handled to prevent unsafe or premature discharges.

Lack of communication with each other. I had a tech administer the same test as triage that was not ordered again. A nurse who requested urine from me twice after providing it less than 30 mins of the request. I told her another nurse took the sample, she seemed very confused. During my care they didn’t seem to have much to offer me in terms of possible test and diagnosis to rule out my symptoms. Everyone is concerned about moving fast and not providing efficient and effective treatment to help aid in my concerns. I also had someone with a tablet ask me to fill out paperwork while in the emergency room. I was also moved from one bed to a random corner. I’m very sad and disappointed. I’ve been experiencing symptoms for a while and thought NYU Langone could help provide some clarity.

Today was a pretty bad day for my health, but still there was no excuse for the way that I treated this nurse. Her name was Emma she was so kind to me and I lost it yelling and screaming and cursing at her and I made her cry. I am so sorry for my actions even with everything I put her through she still with tears in her eyes held her compulsion and the only thing she said was that was not nice what you said to me. Dear nurse Emma my please forgive me and thank you for all your kindness and being an amazing nurse. Also thank you to everyone down in the MRI room for helping me remain calm I would also like to thank nurse Orlando for his kindness and great service at NYU langone. Even though I suffer with mental problems the entire staff remained calm, loving, empathetic and caring. This is the number one Hospital in the world. So if you ever put on 17 West you don't have to worry about a thing you will be taken care of properly. I am feeling so much better they definitely have healing hands. Written with love and appreciation Ms King

I have several reviews in one. The reason that I gave it a 2 is because the first experience was very pleasant, and very clean. I had to bring my mother here on June 12, 2023 until July 7,2023 but she was rushed to find an facility,  and she was in 17 WEST. Although she did get 3 bed sores and several blisters, the nurses were mostly pleasant, and the rooms, floors etc were cleaned regularly (Several times a day)

Then because the facility was horrible (BRONX GARDENS REHABILITATION AND NURSING ,A CITIDEL COMPANY), so she is back. This time she was sent to 17 East and it is disgusting. I asked the nurse , is the maintenance was going to mop the floor or clean up, and I was told that they do not clean with patients in the room. That is disgusting. So I started cleaning myself with the Clorox and Alcohol wipes. Finally I started asking the maintenance to PLEASE clean the floor and he started doing it regularly. The doctor that she has this week is at best adequate but did not even want to check her, even though she stated that she believed that she had a stroke two days ago. The social workers Edouard/ and Marta are rude and disrespectful. I talked to Robert Valley from patient relations about construction being done 10 feet away from her room in this well over 100 year building, about asbestos concerns and his response was “Well I come here and I am not worried” the truth is, he should be because it is a long term concern . The makeshift Wall with the door that constantly opens is surely not protecting the patients from asbestos and every other entity that is in these ancient buildings. Patient relations is just here to patronize you and give you a false sense of security. There was a nasty patient care technician and two VERY NASTY NURSES. They brush it off.

UPDATE
There is a social worker MARTA WHO  IS VERY UNPROFESSIONAL!!!! She gave my mother discharge papers that included a sheet that said that she was in need of a Level 2 evaluation and wanted her to sign it. My mother waited for me to sign, and I CAN ATTEST TO THE FACT THAT SHE DID NOT HAVE A LEVEL 2 EVALUATION. I asked her what it was and she told me to cross it out. When she gave me the same paperwork yesterday that page was not given, Which means one of two things. Either she snatched it out, or today when the paperwork was printed it seen a need for a level 2 evaluation. She is the worse social worker in the hospital by far. So she then goes out the room talking about me to the nurses/ volunteers, and patient care techs. Grow up. She should be fired for her actions, but I am certain that this hospital does not care. I will NEVER BRING MY MOTHER BACK TO TISCH.

Delivered my baby boy here. My doctors and nurses were awesome!! But the facility is so lacking and just dirty. Photos are a bit decieving and while they've redone the lobby, it feels like they slapped lipstick on a pig. The delivery / OB floor rooms have not been updated since the 70s. The sinks have stains, the floor does and overall just run down.
Wish I had checked it out before coming, would not recommend on delivering here especially with what they charge your insurance.
